title: Collecting robot training data is dirty, unglamorous work. Some AI labs are already paying XDOF to do it.
slides: Who wants to clean robot training data?
AI labs are paying XDOF for this work.
But XDOF isn't what you think it is.
You can now skip the dirtiest collection step.
Stop paying for data—start paying for outcomes.
thread_text:
1. Who pays for data?

AI labs spent over $2M on human labelers last year—but the real cost is what you can’t clean.

2. XDOF isn't acronym.

XDOF is a market—buyers set the price, workers set the speed. You never touch raw data.

3. The hidden rot.

Most training data contains NSFW, hate speech, or duplicate entries. XDOF filters that out before you see it.

4. Skip the mess.

Your team spends 40% of budget on collection and cleaning. XDOF cuts that to zero upfront.

5. Your move.

Take one prompt you’d normally hand to a labeler and run it through XDOF. Did you just skip a week of cleanup?
video_script: Spent millions on data and still need cleaning.
XDOF isn't a company—it's a new market.
No more seeing the dirtiest raw data.
Stop paying per row, start paying per result.
Try one prompt on XDOF and see the difference.

instagram.caption:
AI labs have already spent over $2M on human labelers last year—and that’s just the start of the rot.

Most training data is full of NSFW, hate speech, and duplicates. You never see that mess. A new market called XDOF lets buyers skip collection entirely by paying for outcomes instead. 🗑️

Your team can now skip the dirtiest cleanup step and stop paying for raw data. 💡

Go run one prompt you normally hand to a labeler through XDOF—did you just dodge a week of grunt work? 🎯
